expectations.text: Response has three sections: Architecture Overview, Code Implementation, Review Points
expectations.evidence: The document has exactly three sections: '## 1. Architecture Overview' (line 3), '## 2. Code Implementation' (line 51), '## 3. Review Points' (line 628).

expectations.text: Types/Schemas, Hooks, and Components are separated into distinct files or clearly labeled sections
expectations.evidence: Types are in 'features/users/types.ts', hooks are in separate files under 'hooks/' (use-users-filter.ts, use-users.ts, use-debounced-value.ts), and components are in separate files under 'components/'. Within the Code Implementation section, they are also organized under '### Types / Schemas', '### Hooks', and '### Components' subsections.

expectations.text: No usage of 'any' type in TypeScript code
expectations.evidence: Searched all code blocks in the response. No instance of the 'any' type appears anywhere. Types are properly defined using Zod inference (z.infer), explicit interfaces, and proper generics (e.g., useDebouncedValue<T>).

expectations.text: Uses shadcn/ui components (e.g., Table, Input, Select)
expectations.evidence: Multiple shadcn/ui components are imported and used: Table/TableBody/TableCell/TableHead/TableHeader/TableRow from '@/components/ui/table', Input from '@/components/ui/input', Select/SelectContent/SelectItem/SelectTrigger/SelectValue from '@/components/ui/select', Badge from '@/components/ui/badge', Button from '@/components/ui/button', Skeleton from '@/components/ui/skeleton', Pagination components from '@/components/ui/pagination'.

expectations.text: Each code block includes a file path comment
expectations.evidence: Every code block starts with a file path comment: '// features/users/types.ts', '// features/users/hooks/use-users-filter.ts', '// features/users/hooks/use-users.ts', '// features/users/hooks/use-debounced-value.ts', '// features/users/components/users-page.tsx', '// features/users/components/users-toolbar.tsx', '// features/users/components/users-table.tsx', '// features/users/components/users-pagination.tsx', '// features/users/components/users-table-skeleton.tsx', '// app/users/page.tsx'.

expectations.text: Uses TanStack Query (useQuery or useSuspenseQuery) for data fetching
expectations.evidence: In 'features/users/hooks/use-users.ts', useSuspenseQuery and queryOptions are imported from '@tanstack/react-query' and used: 'export function useUsers(filters: UsersFilterParams) { return useSuspenseQuery(usersQueryOptions(filters)); }'

expectations.text: Search, pagination, and filter logic are encapsulated in custom hooks
expectations.evidence: useUsersFilter hook (use-users-filter.ts) encapsulates search, pagination, and role filter state management via URL params. useUsers hook (use-users.ts) encapsulates data fetching with those filter params. useDebouncedValue hook (use-debounced-value.ts) encapsulates search debounce logic. The setFilters callback in useUsersFilter handles page reset when search/role changes.

expectations.text: Uses Zod schema for API response validation
expectations.evidence: UsersResponseSchema is defined using z.object() in types.ts (lines 83-91), and in use-users.ts the API response is validated with 'UsersResponseSchema.parse(json)' (line 200). The Review Points section also explicitly discusses this: 'API 응답을 UsersResponseSchema.parse()로 검증한다'.
